intent_questions.question: What behavior must be proven?
intent_questions.self_answer: GET / from the README Spring Boot Example controller should return HTTP 200 and the exact body Hello World!.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may test a different route, infer JSON, or skip the text body contract.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Replace the route, port, and expected output only with exact public repository evidence.

intent_questions.question: What is the system boundary: Runner target, Mocker dependency, hook host, or configuration-as-code?
intent_questions.self_answer: The Runner target is an externally-started Spring Boot HTTP process on 127.0.0.1:8080; QaaS YAML does not start or clean that process.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: Process lifecycle could be incorrectly encoded as undocumented QaaS YAML.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide documented QaaS lifecycle support or keep startup in an external harness plan.

intent_questions.question: What public docs/schema path proves the capability exists?
intent_questions.self_answer: QaaS public docs/schema support HTTP Transactions, FromFileSystem data-source payload bytes, HttpStatus assertions, and custom assertions by short type name; the ExactHttpTextBody hook is authored but not schema/template/live validated.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may invent a built-in text body assertion or treat sidecar hook code as executable validation.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide template/build/live validation for the custom assertion packet.

intent_questions.question: What inputs, outputs, and side effects prove success?
intent_questions.self_answer: Input is a GET request to /. Outputs are HTTP status 200 and body Hello World!. Side effects should be read-only for this GET.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may over-assert mutations, persistence, or unrelated endpoints.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide public side-effect evidence for any additional assertion.

intent_questions.question: Which dependencies must exist: broker, HTTP endpoint, database, Redis, S3, Kubernetes, filesystem, credentials, ports?
intent_questions.self_answer: Dependencies are JDK, selected Spring Boot dependency/build metadata, a built application JAR, and port 8080 availability; those dependencies are identified from public evidence but not live-validated.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may be promoted without a real build or process lifecycle.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide exact build metadata and validated lifecycle transcripts for the target environment.

intent_questions.question: What can run now, and what must be deferred?
intent_questions.self_answer: Static candidate validation can run now. Dependency resolution, JAR build, process lifecycle, custom assertion build/schema/template validation, live act/assert execution, cleanup proof, and live weak-model validation are deferred and block promotion.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may confuse a candidate packet with an executable Spring Boot test.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Attach passing dependency, build, lifecycle, custom assertion, live, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence.

intent_questions.question: Which negative, malformed, outage, retry, cleanup, and observability cases matter?
intent_questions.self_answer: Negative, malformed, retry, outage, and observability cases remain blocked; this candidate covers only one README-backed happy path plus cleanup planning.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may claim broad Spring Boot framework coverage from one teaser route.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add exact public negative-case and observability contracts before expanding assertions.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The README Example controller is the public behavior contract for this candidate.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: It contains exact controller annotations, root route, SpringApplication.run, and body markers harvested from immutable Git blobs.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may not match another Spring Boot application.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Replace the selected contract with another immutable public blob.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The repository webserver docs prove the standalone default HTTP port 8080.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The selected webserver doc contains an exact default-port marker harvested from an immutable Git blob.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A customized app may run on another port, so executable promotion still requires lifecycle evidence.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide exact port evidence or configure a live harness override before promotion.

intent_assumptions.assumption: Plain-text body comparison is represented by a custom assertion sidecar, but remains blocked until build/schema/template/live validation passes.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The available built-ins do not prove exact raw text equality, while public custom assertion docs support sidecar authoring by short type name.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may treat authored code as runtime validation.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide passing custom assertion build, schema, template, and live QaaS evidence.

cases.scenario: Call README-backed Spring Boot GET / on documented default port 8080 and assert HTTP 200.
setup: Build and start the Spring Boot application externally only after dependency and JAR evidence exists.
Use get-root.bin as the byte payload driver for the GET transaction.
act: QaaS Runner sends GET / to http://127.0.0.1:8080.
assert: Exact body Hello World! after ExactHttpTextBody is build/template/live validated.
cleanup: Terminate the tracked Java process tree.
Remove candidate session-data after live validation.

cases.scenario: Keep Java process startup, JAR build, and cleanup outside QaaS YAML until public QaaS docs prove lifecycle support.
action: Build a JAR from selected public dependencies.
Start and stop Java through a tracked process runner, not through undocumented QaaS YAML fields.

cases.scenario: Tie every candidate field to immutable selected public contract evidence and QaaS public docs.
setup: Review selected-contract.json, README.adoc blob, webserver docs blob, and QaaS schema docs.

blocked_reason: Selected Spring Boot candidate passed lifecycle, custom ExactHttpTextBody build/template/live validation, and QaaS template/live validation, but executable promotion remains blocked until live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review pass.
coverage_notes: Candidate uses only README-backed controller/root/body evidence and repository docs default-port evidence.
Candidate preserves README-backed plain-text body evidence and includes a sidecar ExactHttpTextBody custom assertion packet that is not wired into active YAML before validation.
Candidate does not claim broad Spring Boot framework coverage.

builtins_considered: HttpStatus validates status code only and does not prove exact raw body text.
OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults is JSON/CSV oriented and is not used for a raw Spring Boot text response.
ObjectOutputJsonSchema is not applicable to a plain text body.

dependency_gates.blocked_reason: Live weak-model route has not passed this candidate.

validation_sequence: Validate selected public Git blob evidence.
Select exact Java/Spring Boot dependency and build metadata from public evidence.
Build an application JAR from selected public dependencies.
Build and schema/template-validate the sidecar ExactHttpTextBody custom assertion before executable promotion.
Run tracked Java process lifecycle and cleanup validation.
Run live QaaS act/assert against tracked Spring Boot process.
Run strong review against selected public evidence.

findings: Source YAML does not start Java or activate the custom assertion before validation.
Dependency resolution and JAR build are explicit blockers.
Exact body is preserved in sidecar artifacts and expected-body hash.

source_only_blockers.description: Live weak-model validation has not passed for this candidate packet.
required_evidence: Airgapped transcript with dry_run false and expected output contract preserved
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Run D:\QaaS\_tools\weak-model-session.ps1 -Airgapped -All -ReasoningEffort none when quota/routing is available.

source_only_blockers.description: This candidate covers only the README Example root route and does not test the broader Spring Boot framework or application variants.
required_evidence: Additional selected public contracts for negative, configuration, actuator, servlet, and reactive behavior
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Add separate selected public contracts and candidate packets for additional Spring Boot surfaces.

required_evidence: Public API, CLI, or runtime contract
C# build result when code artifacts exist
Live QaaS run/act/assert result when dependency gates are ready

lifecycle_validation.command: Spring Initializr 4.0.6 ; mvnw.cmd -q -Dmaven.test.skip=true package ; java -jar <built jar> ; GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/ ; terminate tracked process tree

validation_advisories.description: The quick-start example uses stale HttpStatus field names, while generated schema docs require StatusCode and OutputNames.
validation_advisories.resolved_by: schema-derived StatusCode/OutputNames template and live validation
